witnesses.description: Two overlapping finite subsets used to replay inclusion-exclusion.
Two disjoint finite subsets whose union count is additive.
A finite bipartite edge table used for double-counting.
The full powerset of a three-element set.
A concrete overlap counterexample to disjoint-union additivity.

checks.claim: The listed finite sets satisfy two-set inclusion-exclusion.
checks.notes: The validator recomputes the union, intersection, and all listed counts.

checks.claim: The listed disjoint finite sets satisfy additive cardinality.
checks.notes: The validator checks disjointness and recomputes the union count.

checks.claim: The sum of left degrees equals the sum of right degrees and both count the same finite edge set.
checks.notes: The validator recomputes degrees from the listed edge table.

checks.claim: The listed powerset of a three-element set has 2^3 elements.
checks.notes: The validator enumerates all subsets of the base set and compares them to the listed table.

checks.claim: The formula |A union B| = |A| + |B| is valid for arbitrary finite sets.
checks.notes: The validator checks the overlap and confirms the false disjoint sum differs from the true union count.

checks.claim: The overlapping sets also satisfy the disjoint-additivity equality |A union B| = |A| + |B|.
checks.notes: After finite set replay computes |A union B| = 4 and |A| + |B| = 6, Axeyum checks the final equality contradiction through QF_LIA/Diophantine evidence.

checks.claim: Arbitrary set cardinality theorems such as Cantor-Schroeder-Bernstein require a theorem-prover route.

data.future_checker: Lean module proving the theorem without sorryAx

checks.notes: Finite cardinality rows are checked; arbitrary infinite-cardinality theorems remain Lean horizon.
